mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS reduces the equity risk premium hurdle and lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, generating a structural tailwind for S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as rates remain pinned near the effective lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.06 as of 2021-05, near effective lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns are not significantly higher than unconditional mean when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market healing that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.8 in 2021-05, declining from pandemic peak of ~14.8
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns does not exceed 55%, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL month-over-month combined with a policy rate (FEDFUNDS) pinned near zero indicates that real rates are becoming increasingly negative, raising market expectations of premature Fed tightening and repricing equity valuations downward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=268.551 in 2021-05 rising amid near-zero FEDFUNDS=0.06
failure_condition: The joint signal (CPI acceleration + near-zero funds rate) fails to produce negative mean 20-day forward returns, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from the sum of individual single-series effects (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-pandemic-shock recovery regime, month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS reflects pent-up consumer demand release that is quickly priced into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon immediately following the retail sales release.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=620214 million in 2021-05, elevated post-stimulus consumer spending
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with fiscal stimulus support
failure_condition: Short-horizon returns following positive RSAFS surprises are not significantly different from returns following flat/negative surprises (p > 0.05), or effect reverses sign outside the stimulus-driven recovery window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in INDPRO signals expanding industrial capacity utilization and improving corporate earnings power, which is incorporated into equity prices with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly (60-day) horizon whenever INDPRO's 3-month trend is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=99.8562 in 2021-05, recovering toward pre-pandemic 2017=100 baseline
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO trend and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS shows month-over-month job gains exceeding 500,000 concurrently with UNRATE declining, the joint signal indicates a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days, an effect stronger than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=144894 thousand in 2021-05, still below pre-pandemic peak, recovering with UNRATE=5.8 declining
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal's rank IC is not meaningfully higher than the better-performing single-series model (delta IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST exceeds 1,500 thousand annualized units for two consecutive months, it signals overheating housing demand that raises inflation and rate-hike expectations, causing equity markets to price in tighter future monetary policy and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1569 thousand units in 2021-04, above pre-pandemic norms
regime_hint: housing demand overheating amid ultra-loose policy
failure_condition: Instances where the HOUST trigger fires do not show negative mean 5-day forward returns, or the effect is not statistically distinct from zero (p > 0.05) across at least 3 triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime, if CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 4%, the market begins to discount an eventual hawkish policy pivot, causing a repricing of long-duration equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon that would not occur under normal policy regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=268.551 in 2021-05 with FEDFUNDS=0.06, YoY inflation approaching multi-decade highs
regime_hint: zero-rate policy regime facing emergent inflation risk
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following CPI YoY breaches above 4% under near-zero FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses when FEDFUNDS is above 1%, indicating the mechanism is not regime-specific.
